description_long: This stone castle features a circular central keep surrounded by a fortified wall, punctuated by four cylindrical corner towers with conical slate roofs. The walls are constructed from light gray, roughly hewn stone blocks, giving the structure a robust and ancient appearance. The towers are evenly spaced and rise above the main wall, providing elevated vantage points. The castle sits atop a textured, rocky base that mimics natural terrain, enhancing its imposing presence. Small, rectangular windows and battlements are visible along the walls, suggesting defensive capabilities. The overall design is reminiscent of medieval European fortifications, blending both functional and decorative architectural elements.
description: A circular stone castle with four tall corner towers, conical roofs, and battlemented walls, set on a rocky base. The structure is detailed with stone textures and defensive features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, central keep, and all four towers with conical roofs clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the curvature of the wall, three prominent towers, and the rocky base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the central keep, two towers, and the continuous stone wall.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizing the circular layout, three towers, and the stonework details.
